content: postMessage sender must always specify the exact targetOrigin, never '*'. Receiver must validate event.origin (and optionally event.source) before trusting a message -- a common broken pattern is a loose regex/substring match on origin (e.g. /safe.example.com/) which an attacker can defeat with a lookalike domain (safe.example.com.attacker.com). All incoming message data must be treated as untrusted and validated/sanitized (never piped into innerHTML/eval) even after origin check passes.
